title: ranunculus pyrenaeus subsp. pyrenaeus
intro: Ranunculus pyrenaeus subsp. pyrenaeus is a plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, commonly known as the lesser celandine or Pyrenean buttercup. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus pyrenaeus subsp. pyrenaeus include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
